title: NEPSE Index Dips By 8.30 Points to Close at 2,653.40 Levels; Finance Index Gains the Highest Among Other Indices
content:
The Nepal Stock Exchange (NEPSE) recorded a fall today, falling by 8.30 points (0.31%) to settle at 2,653.40.This comes after a gain of 8.78 points in the previous trading session.

The benchmark index opened at 2673.15 and fluctuated between an intraday high of 2,673.14 and a low of 2,646.78 before settling at the close. Total turnover for the day amounted to Rs. 4.41 Arba, with 8,584,401 shares traded across 345 companies through 46,234 transactions. The total market capitalization stood at Rs. 45.59 Kharba, with a float market capitalization of Rs. 15.37 Kharba.

4% Agricultural Bond (ADBLB) topped the turnover chart, recording transactions worth Rs. 43 crores. The company’s stock closed at Rs. 1,000.00.

Upper Lohore Khola Hydropower Company Limited (ULHC), Snow Rivers Limited (SNORL) Led the gainer's list with 15.00% positive circuits.

Meanwhile, Upper Lohore Khola Hydropower Company Limited (ULHC) recorded the Highest fall, falling by 13.65%, the last traded price of the company was Rs. 429.00 .

Among sectors, Finance Index registered the highest gain by 1.06%, while Banking SubIndex recorded highest loss, gaining by 0.96%.

title: Citizens Mutual Fund 2 Delisted From NEPSE; Fund to Be Matured From Ashad 21
content:
Citizens Mutual Fund 2 (CMF2), a closed-end 7-year mutual fund scheme with a total size of Rs. 56 crores, will mature in 21st of Ashad, 2083.

Managed by Citizens Capital Limited with Citizens Bank International Limited as the fund sponsor, this fund issued its delisting notice on the 19th of Ashad, 2083. Notably, the scheme has consistently provided dividends to investors over the past six fiscal years.

After the end of the maturity period, the transaction of the shares of the mutual fund will be suspended and the investors are to receive their share of investment after the mutual fund sells all its investment. At the end of its maturity period, the fund needs to sell all the shares, debentures, and non-listed shares and proportionally distribute the amount to the unit holders. The latest NAV stands at Rs. 10.17 as of A.

title: Constitutional Council to Meet Today to Discuss Filling Empty Government Posts
content:
The Constitutional Council is scheduled to meet today at 5:00 PM at the Prime Minister and Council of Ministers' Office.

According to Bhishma Raj Angdembe, the chief opposition leader and head of the Nepali Congress parliamentary committee, the meeting will focus on recommending names to fill empty positions in various national constitutional bodies. He confirmed that the official agenda shared for the meeting centers around selecting individuals for these vacant posts.

title: Nepal Health Insurance Board Pays Rs 23.44 Billion to Hospitals; Services Set to Resume
content:
The Health Insurance Board of Nepal has announced that it paid 23.44 billion NPR (specifically Rs 23,44,02,98,016) to participating hospitals and healthcare institutions during the current fiscal year. Following this massive release of funds, hospitals that had previously halted or suspended their services under the government's health insurance program have started restarting them.

According to a public notice issued by the Board, this payment completely clears all outstanding insurance claims made by healthcare providers up to the mid-December 2025 (end of Mangsir 2082). The Board acknowledged that limited resources, structural hurdles, and the lack of a fully secured, permanent funding system had previously delayed payments. This delay had caused significant challenges for both medical institutions and the citizens relying on the insurance scheme.

To address claims made after mid-December 2025, the Board stated it is actively working and coordinating with higher authorities to secure the necessary budget and long-term financial sources.

Finally, the Board has urged all partner hospitals to continue providing high-quality medical services to insured patients without any interruptions or difficulties. At the same time, it has appealed to the public to use their health insurance responsibly. Citizens are requested not to demand unnecessary medical tests or pressure doctors into providing unneeded treatments, helping to ensure the insurance program remains sustainable for everyone.

title: Nepal Government to Fine Petrol Pumps Up to Rs 10 Lakh for Creating Artificial Fuel Shortages
content:
The government has introduced strict penalties for petrol pumps that create an artificial shortage of fuel, with fines reaching up to Rs 10 lakh (1 million rupees).

To crack down on these unfair practices, the Ministry of Industry, Commerce and Supplies has officially amended the rules under the Nepal Oil Corporation's 'Petroleum Products Sellers Bylaws'. The new rules outline a three-tier penalty system depending on how many times a gas station repeats the offense.

According to the updated regulations, a petrol pump caught creating an artificial fuel shortage for the first time will face a fine of Rs 3 lakh. Additionally, the government will stop its fuel supply for 5 days.

If the gas station is caught committing the same offense a second time, the penalty escalates to a fine of Rs 6 lakh, along with a 10-day ban on its fuel supply.

For a third offense, the punishment becomes even more severe. The station will be hit with a maximum fine of Rs 10 lakh, and its fuel supply will be completely cut off for 15 days.

title: Nepali Life Expectancy Hits 71.3 Years as Literacy Climbs to 76.2%
content:
The National Statistics Office (NSO) has released a comprehensive infographics report detailing Nepal’s latest economic and social indicators. Drawing from the National Census 2021 (2078 BS), the Nepal Living Standards Survey 2023/24 (2079/80 BS), and the National Agricultural Census 2021, the report shows strong progress in social sectors alongside persistent economic challenges.

Population and Social Changes

Nepal's total population stands at 29,164,578, with a slowing annual growth rate of 0.92%. Life expectancy has risen remarkably to 71.3 years (68.2 years for men and 73.8 years for women). The working age population (15 to 59 years) accounts for 61.96% of the total, though job shortages continue to drive migration; over 2.19 million individuals from more than 1.55 million families currently live abroad. Additionally, 60% of Nepali households now live as nuclear families, and the average age for first marriage has reached 20 years.

Poverty and Economy

According to the latest Living Standards Survey, 20.27% of Nepal’s population lives below the poverty line. Poverty is higher in rural areas (24.66%) compared to urban areas (18.34%). Sudurpashchim Province records the highest poverty rate at 34.16%, while Bagmati Province records the lowest at 12.59%. Under the new threshold, individuals spending less than Rs 72,908 per year are classified as poor. Under the Multidimensional Poverty Index (MPI), 17.4% of Nepalis are poor.

For the fiscal year 2080/81, the economy is estimated to reach Rs 5.704 trillion, with a preliminary growth rate of 3.87%. Per Capita Gross National Income (GNI) rose to $1,456 from $1,405 the previous year. The service sector dominates the economy at 62.9%, followed by agriculture at 24.6% and industry at 12.5%.

Labor and Agriculture Challenges

The unemployment rate stands at 11.4%, and a worrying 84.6% of employed individuals work in informal sectors, which lack job security. Agriculture is facing fragmentation; land used for farming shrank from 2.525 million hectares in 2011 to 2.218 million hectares in 2021, even though the number of farming families grew to 4.13 million. On the commercial side, Nepal operates 60,185 industrial establishments employing over 491,000 people, and 498,058 trade establishments engaging around 4 million individuals.

title: Nepal Bank Joins NEPALPAY Card Network to Strengthen Digital Payment Services
content:
Nepal Bank Limited and Nepal Clearing House Limited (NCHL) have signed an agreement enabling Nepal Bank to become a principal member of the National Payment Switch (NPS) and NEPALPAY Card scheme. The agreement will allow the bank to issue and accept NEPALPAY Cards after completing system integration, supporting its ongoing digital transformation efforts.

The Nepal Clearing House Limited-led NEPALPAY Card scheme aims to strengthen Nepal’s domestic payment ecosystem by ensuring interoperability, reducing transaction costs, and enabling local processing of card transactions. The card also offers global acceptance through collaboration with Discover Network, while Nepal Bank plans to expand digital banking services and increase card usage nationwide.

title: Muktinath Bikas Bank Expands CSR Support for Students Through Educational Aid Across Nepal
content:
Muktinath Bikas Bank Limited has carried out a nationwide CSR initiative aimed at promoting education and supporting students in government schools across Nepal. Under the program, the bank distributed school uniforms, school bags, stationery materials, educational equipment, sports materials, and financial support for one academic year to students in multiple districts, including Biratnagar, Bardibas, Sandhikharka, and Butwal.

The bank directly supported hundreds of students, including children with disabilities and six financially vulnerable children affected by fire incidents, covering their annual education expenses in partnership with Astitwa Nepal. It also donated digital smart boards, ceiling fans, and sports materials to several schools, aiming to improve learning opportunities for economically disadvantaged students.

title: Garima Bikas Bank Wins Silver Award at ICAN Best Presented Annual Report Award 2025
content:
Garima Bikas Bank Limited has received the Silver Award at the Best Presented Annual Report (BPA) Award 2025, presented annually by the Institute of Chartered Accountants of Nepal (ICAN). The award recognizes transparency, accountability, and good governance standards in annual reports, with Garima Bikas Bank being honored in the development bank category based on its FY 2081/82 annual report.

The award was handed over in Kathmandu by Nil Bahadur Saru Magar to the bank’s CEO, Madhav Prasad Upadhaya, and Accounts Head Keshav Timilsina. The bank has also recently won “Best Managed Development Bank 2026” and “Development Bank of the Year 2026” awards. Currently, Garima Bikas Bank serves nearly 900,000 customers through 126 branches, 51 ATMs, and digital banking services across Nepal.

title: Sanima Bank Conducts Health Camps and Nutrition Support for Pregnant and Postpartum Women Nationwide
content:
Sanima Bank Limited has launched a nationwide Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) initiative to support pregnant and postpartum women across all seven provinces of Nepal. The program focused on improving maternal and newborn health by addressing issues such as malnutrition, anemia, and other pregnancy-related health complications.

Under the campaign, the bank coordinated with local municipalities and ward offices to directly benefit around 1,050 women, providing health check-ups by medical professionals and distributing three months’ supply of nutritious food packages. The bank said it will continue to implement social programs that have a positive impact on targeted communities. Currently, Sanima Bank operates 134 branches and 127 ATMs across Nepal.

title: Bhanu Literary Park Construction Gains Momentum, Rs. 240 Million Allocated for First Phase
content:
The construction of infrastructure for 'Bhanu Sahitya Udyan' (Bhanu Literary Park), a project of national pride aimed at transforming Chundi Ramgha, the birthplace of Nepali poet Bhanu Bhakta Acharya (first poet of Nepali literature), into an international academic and literary hub, has made significant progress.

The project is being developed across nearly 500 ropanis of land at Ramgha Danda in Bhanu Municipality–4, Tanahun, with construction work commencing under the first phase following the allocation of the initial budget.

According to the approved master plan, the literary park will include key infrastructure such as a modern Bhanu Bhavan, a full-sized statue of Aadikavi Bhanu Bhakta Acharya, Saptadham, Sahitya Gram, Pragya Bhavan, and a technology-enabled ticketing system. The park will also feature replicas of important religious and cultural heritage sites from all seven provinces, along with statues of renowned figures from Nepali literature.

Bhanu Municipality Mayor Anandraj Tripathi said the Initial Environmental Examination (IEE) report has been finalized by the Department of Urban Development and Building Construction to ensure the project is implemented in an environmentally sustainable and well-managed manner. He informed that the government has allocated Rs. 240 million for the project in the upcoming fiscal year through the Department of Urban Development and Building Construction. The budget will be utilized for infrastructure development under a multi-year implementation plan.

The initiative to establish the Bhanu Literary Park was launched under the vision and special initiative of President Ramchandra Paudel. Recognized as a national pride project, the foundation stone of the park was laid by President Paudel on April 27, marking the beginning of a landmark effort to preserve and promote Nepal's literary heritage while developing the site into a major cultural and tourism destination.

title: 100% Right Shares of Buddha Bhumi Nepal Hydropower Company Limited Listed on NEPSE
content:
40,00,000 unit right shares of Buddha Bhumi Nepal Hydropower Company Limited (BNHC) has been listed in NEPSE.

The company issued a 1:1 ratio right share i.e. 40 lakhs units to its shareholders from 22nd Chaitra to 13th Baisakh, 2083. Out of which only 35,25,304 units were allotted to the eligible shareholders of the company. The remaining 4,74,696 units of unsubscribed right shares were later sold through an auction from the 21st Jestha to 29th Jestha , 2083.

The same 40,00,000 units of right shares are now listed in NEPSE.

NIC Asia Capital Limited was the company's auction manager.

As of writing, BNHC has an LTP of Rs. 297.20 .

title: Lawmakers Push For Transparent Inquiry Into Ganesh Nepali's Death
content:
Lawmakers have demanded an impartial investigation into the self immolation and subsequent death of Ganesh Nepali, 25, of Mugu.

Nepali had committed self immolation at Tripureshwor, Kathmandu on July 9. Soon after the commencement of meeting of the House of Representatives today, Speaker Dol Prasad Aryal allocated time to lawmaker Basana Thapa to put forth her views after lawmakers of the opposition parties protested by standing up from their respective seats. On the occasion, Thapa questioned why the government remained silent on Nepal's self-immolation attempt on Thursday.

She argued that no one's safety is ensured by changing green buses into blue ones, adding the government has created environment that compels landless squatters to finish themselves. Bhumika Limbu Subba drew government's attention, saying Ganesh Nepali had attempted self immolation after he was fined forRs 10,000for parking motorcycle.

Parbati BK asked the government to provide compensation to the deceased's family while Radhika Ramtel demanded investigation to the incident. Gyan Bahadur Shahi asked the government to create environment where even poor people could survive easily in the capital city. Speaking in the urgent hour of the meeting, Khadka Bahadur Shahi and Barsha Man Pun expressed sorrow over the death of Ganesh Nepali, saying it is tragic that Ganesh Nepali chose the self-immolation.

Ashika Tamang, Yashoda Kumari Baral and Shahajan Khautn complained that the state turned its deaf ear towards the self immolation incident. Saying Ganesh Nepali died in course of treatment at Bir Hospital after self immolation attempt, Ain Mahar wondered why the family members of Ganesh were not allowed to meet him. Gita Gurung drew government's attention on the issue of landless squatters.

content:

The benchmark index opened at 2696.61 and fluctuated between an intraday high of 2,623.24 and a low of 2,592.67 before settling at the close. The total market capitalization stood at Rs. 44.68 Kharba, with a float market capitalization of Rs. 15.08 Kharba.

title: Sun Securities Conducts Orientation Program on Nepal's Capital Market for IIDS Trainees
content:
Sun Securities Private Limited (Broker No. 64) has successfully conducted an orientation and interactive training program on Nepal's capital market for Nepali and international trainees from the Institute for Integrated Development Studies (IIDS).

The program was held at Sun Securities's trading hall in Narayanhiti, Naxal, Kathmandu, from 10:00 AM to 12:30 PM, with the participation of around 35 trainees. The event saw notable participation from trainees of various nationalities alongside their Nepali counterparts. The program was facilitated by Mr. Krishna Giri Executive Chairman of  Sun Securities Pvt. Ltd., Mr. Astitwa Sharma CEO of Subiz Grou, and Mr. Bibek Adhikari Trading Officer of Sun Securities Pvt. Ltd.

The training covered a comprehensive overview of Nepal's capital market, the functioning of the Nepal Stock Exchange (NEPSE), use of the ATrade (new TMS) system, use of Meroshare and Demat accounts, basic investment principles, key considerations before investing, balancing risk and return, and the role of the capital market in long-term wealth creation.

Participants showed strong enthusiasm and active engagement throughout the session, raising numerous questions. International trainees, in particular, expressed keen interest in the structure of Nepal's capital market, the investment process, and the technical systems involved, actively participating in the interactive discussion. Through such educational and awareness programs, Sun Securities Pvt. Ltd. has reaffirmed its ongoing commitment to enhancing financial literacy among students, youth, and the general public, and to fostering a culture of responsible investment.

title: Minister Chaudhary Urges Expansion of Youth Employment and Volunteer Programs
content:
Minister for Agriculture, Forests and Environment, Gita Chaudhary, has instructed all departments and bodies under the Ministry to work in result oriented manner by expanding youth targeted employment, paid training and volunteer opportunities for youths.

During the meeting with chiefs of Nepal Agriculture Research Council (NARC), Dairy Development Cooperation, Herbs Production and Processing Company Limited, Department of Hydrology and Meteorology as well as other subordinate departments and bodies today, Minister Chaudhary mentioned that activities should be carried out making the citizens realize about it, as youths have trusted the incumbent government. She underscored that she was ready to make legal and policy level arrangements for this purpose.

Minister Chaudhary directed the line departments and bodies under the Ministry to prepare details and action plans on how many youths they could provide employment to, paid training and volunteer service, and submit the proposal at the Ministry within this month. Urging to work in an implementation and result oriented manner, she stressed the need of developing agriculture, forests and environment sector as an attractive employment sector for youths.

On the occasion, departmental chiefs shared that paid and unpaid programmes as well as volunteer programme are currently being run under their offices across the country. Though initiatives were taken to involve students in 'internship' collaborating with different universities, it has not happened due to some legal provisions, they opined.

title: Xiaomi Unveils Everest Expedition Video Shot on Xiaomi 17 Ultra Filmed by Gelje Sherpa
content:
Xiaomi has just released a breathtaking video shot on the video shot on the Xiaomi 17 Ultra by world-renowned mountaineer Gelje Sherpa  during his Mount Everest expedition in May 2026.

The footage was filmed in extreme cold, low oxygen levels, and strong winds. It captures unstage authentic real-time visuals from the actual climb, with no staged scenes or added effects. Gelje Sherpa, a record-holding climber and a Guinness World Record holder, documented the expedition himself using the smartphone.

The video gives viewers a rare and honest look at the challenges of high-altitude mountaineering. It also showcases the Xiaomi 17 Ultra's ability to produce high-quality visuals in some of the world's toughest conditions.

For Xiaomi, this project is more than just a product showcase. By choosing Mount Everest as its testing ground, the company aims to demonstrate the durability, reliability, and camera performance of the Xiaomi 17 Ultra in extreme conditions. This reflects their broader message that technology can help people achieve remarkable goals.

title: Ganesh Nepali, Man Who Attempted Self Immolation in Kathmandu, Passes Away
content:
Ganesh Nepali, a 25 year old ride share driver who attempted self immolation in Tirpureshwor Kathmandu, near passport department, has passed away while undergoing treatment at Bir Hospital.

The tragedy unfolded on Thursday afternoon outside the Department of Passports in Tripureshwar. Nepali, a resident of Soru Rural Municipality-1 in Mugu. The city police clamped a wheel lock on his motorcycle over an alleged parking violation. In a moment of extreme distress, Nepali siphoned petrol from his fuel tank, poured it over himself, and set himself on fire.

Though nearby police personnel acted quickly to put out the flames and rushed him to Bir Hospital, doctors stated that Nepali had sustained deep burns covering 55% to 60% of his body. The fire severely damaged his face, head, neck, chest, and arms, while also heavily compromising his respiratory tract.

title: MetLife Nepal Earns Great Place To Work Certification for Second Consecutive Year
content:
MetLife Nepal has been certified as a Great Place To Work for the second consecutive year, reaffirming its commitment to fostering a positive and employee-centric workplace culture.

The recognition is based entirely on confidential employee feedback, with 87% of employees stating that MetLife Nepal is a great place to work. The certification is awarded by Great Place To Work,a worldwide expert in culture and experience of workplaces. Thanking for the award, MetLife Nepal General Manager Nirmal Kaji Shrestha stated that this recognition is a testimony of employees' trust, commitment, and teamwork.

MetLife has been operating in Nepal since 2001, offering life insurance, accident and health insurance, and employee benefits to individuals and businesses. The company said the recent recognition highlights its continued focus on building an unwavering commitment to cultivating an environment which is inclusive, safe, and based on high levels of trust at work.

title: Nepal Rastra Bank to Collect 60 Billion From Bank and Financial Institution Today
content:
Nepal Rastra Bank (NRB) has announced that it will collect deposits worth Rs. 60 Arba from banks and financial institutions through a bidding process today.

According to a notice published today, the central bank will collect the deposits for a period of 91 days. The bidding will take place on Jestha 26 (July, 10) through the Online Bidding System at 3:00 PM. The interest rate will be decided through the bidding process.

Only licensed ‘A’, ‘B’, and ‘C’ class banks and financial institutions approved by NRB are allowed to take part in the bidding. The deposits collected will be returned along with interest on Ashoj 23, 2083 (October 09, 2026).

title: Kamana Sewa Bikas Bank Donates Water Purifier and Tank to School Under CSR
content:
Under its Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) initiative, Kamana Sewa Bikas Bank donated a water tank and electric water purifier to Tripureshwor Secondary School in Birendranagar, Surkhet.

The support aims to provide students and teachers with access to safe drinking water, improving the school's health and learning environment. Bank officials reaffirmed their commitment to continuing community-focused CSR and awareness programs in collaboration with local stakeholders.

title: Mahalaxmi Bikas Bank Launches Nepal’s First Digital Hub with Coffee Lounge
content:
Mahalaxmi Bikas Bank has officially inaugurated its Digital Hub with Coffee Lounge in Gyaneshwor, Kathmandu, becoming the first bank in Nepal to introduce a café-style digital banking concept. Inaugurated by Board Chairman Rajesh Upadhyaya, the facility offers customers a comfortable space to access services such as cash deposits, withdrawals, cheque clearing, and other digital banking transactions.

The Digital Hub is designed to encourage digital banking adoption, particularly among younger customers, while also providing guidance on the bank’s digital services. The initiative reflects the bank’s commitment to delivering secure, technology-driven, and customer-friendly banking solutions. Inspired by Nepal Rastra Bank’s Regulatory Sandbox concept, the bank also aims to promote financial innovation and introduce new digital services in the future.

title: Minting Multi-Millionaire Investors: Nabil Bank Celebrates 42 Years of Turning Rs 6,000 into Over 25 Million
content:
As one of Nepal's oldest private commercial banks, Nabil Bank has established itself as a true pioneer across multiple financial parameters since its incorporation in 1984. From an investor’s perspective, Nabil has historically topped the commercial banking sector in terms of dividend payouts. For any long-term value investor in the Nepalese stock market, having Nabil Bank (NABIL) in one's portfolio has practically been a given due to this stellar track record.

The Evolution of Capital

During its incorporation in the fiscal year 1984/85, Nabil Bank initially issued 469,707 shares valued at Rs. 60 per share, resulting in an initial paid-up capital of Rs. 28,182,420. The bank continued issuing shares in various lot sizes until FY 1988/89, at which point its paid-up capital reached Rs. 30,000,000 with 500,000 outstanding units.

A major milestone occurred in the fiscal year 1991/92, when Nabil distributed a stock dividend (bonus shares) amounting to Rs. 20,000,000. This pushed the paid-up capital to Rs. 50,000,000 across 500,000 units, effectively adjusting the stock's par value to Rs. 100 per share. As of the fiscal year 2024/25, Nabil Bank's paid-up capital stood at Rs. 27,056,996,729. The bank initially achieved the regulatory minimum milestone of Rs. 8 billion entirely through its own organic earnings. Later, Nabil acquired United Finance Ltd. (UFL) and Nepal Bangladesh Bank Ltd. (NBB), boosting operational synergy and accelerating its business expansion.

An Unmatched Dividend Track Record

Over its 41 years of operation, Nabil Bank has rewarded its shareholders with dividends in 36 of those fiscal years. Remarkably, the bank has maintained an uninterrupted dividend distribution streak for the last 29 consecutive fiscal years.

The Power of Compounding: NABIL as a Real - Life Case Study for Nepalese Investors

To understand the sheer scale of Nabil's wealth generation, consider an initial investment of just Rs. 6,000 used to buy 100 units at the original par value of Rs. 60.

Accounting for decades of bonus shares and cash payouts, as of the Last Traded Price (LTP) on July 10, 2026:

In total, that initial Rs. 6,000 investments would have grown into a staggering Rs. 25,233,333.08. This represents an incredible Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 22.57% a financial return delivered consistently regardless of the socio-economic challenges the nation faced over the decades.

title: Rastriya Banijya Bank Warns Customers Against KYC and OTP Fraud Scams
content:
Rastriya Banijya Bank (RBB) has issued a public warning after receiving reports of fraudsters impersonating bank representatives to deceive customers. The scammers are reportedly calling customers under the pretext of updating KYC details, requesting personal and banking information, asking for OTPs, encouraging screen sharing, installing unauthorized apps, clicking phishing links, or luring victims with fake offers and financial benefits.

The bank clarified that its employees never ask customers for sensitive information such as ATM PINs, mobile banking or e-banking OTPs, passwords, or other confidential details through phone calls, emails, social media, or any other communication channel. RBB has urged customers not to trust such requests and to immediately report any suspicious activity to the nearest branch or through the bank’s official contact numbers. Customers are also advised to rely only on official notices published on the bank’s website. The bank reaffirmed its commitment to ensuring secure banking services and encouraged all customers to remain alert against cyber fraud.

title: Nepal Chamber of Commerce and Bennett University Sign MoU to Boost Entrepreneurship, Innovation, and Skill Development
content:
The Nepal Chamber of Commerce (NCC) and Bennett University of India have signed a five-year Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) to strengthen collaboration in entrepreneurship, innovation, skill development, and industry-academia partnerships. Under the agreement, both institutions will work together to create a shared ecosystem connecting education, industry, startups, investors, and policymakers through training, research, knowledge exchange, startup incubation, leadership development, and investment promotion.

The partnership will offer executive education and specialized training in areas such as artificial intelligence (AI), digital transformation, financial management, supply chain, international trade, and environmental, social, and governance (ESG) practices. It will also support startup acceleration, innovation mentoring, investor conferences, policy research, and future-focused academic programs.

The MoU was signed by NCC Executive Committee Member and Startup & Innovation Committee Chair Pankaj Jalan and Bennett University Chief Operating Officer Senthil Kumar. Speaking at the event, NCC President Kamlesh Kumar Agrawal said the collaboration would deepen Nepal-India relations while equipping Nepali entrepreneurs, startups, and businesses with global knowledge, advanced technologies, and international investment opportunities. Bennett University and NCC representatives expressed confidence that the partnership would strengthen the startup ecosystem, encourage youth entrepreneurship, enhance industrial competitiveness, create employment, and contribute to sustainable economic growth.

title: SEBON Approved Siddhartha Capital’s Mutual Fund Scheme ; "Siddhartha Systematic Investment Plan" To Issue 22 Crore Mutual Fund Units
content:
The Securities Board of Nepal (SEBON) has approved the"Siddhartha Systematic Investment Plan"worth Rs 2.20 arba on Ashad 23, 2083.

This is a closed-end mutual fund scheme that will be floating 22 crore units each share at a par value of 10 per share. The sponsor of the fund is Siddhartha Bank Limited, while the fund manager is Siddhartha Capital Limited.

As of now, Siddhartha Capital is managing three closed-end funds and one open end funds in the market.

title: Ministry of Finance Invites Applications for CEO Position at Deposit and Credit Guarantee Fund
content:
The Government of Nepal has officially launched a search for a new Chief Executive Officer to lead the Deposit and Credit Guarantee Fund (DCGF).

The Ministry of Finance formed a special selection committee and published a vacancy notice on Saturday, inviting qualified Nepali citizens to apply for the top executive position.

According to the announcement, candidates have 21 days from the publication date of Asar 27 to submit their applications. The final deadline for submissions has been set for Shrawan 15, within regular office hours.

Interested applicants must provide an application letter, an updated resume, and certified copies of their educational and work experience certificates. All application packages must be delivered directly to the committee's secretariat, located within the Financial Sector Management and Corporation Coordination Division of the Ministry of Finance in Singha Durbar.

title: Gold Sees Upward Movement Today; Check Today's Trading Rate
content:
Gold prices have witnessed a moderate gain in the domestic market today, gaining by Rs. 600 per tola to settle at Rs.288,000 per tola, up from the previous rate of Rs. 287,400 per tola.

TheFederation of Nepal Gold and Silver Dealers' Association (FENEGOSIDA)reported that Tejabi gold also increases moderately by Rs. 600 per tola to reach Rs. 287,300 per tola, compared to Rs. 286,700 per tola on the previous day.

Similarly, silver prices move in the opposite direction, price falls by Rs. 35 per tola, bringing it to Rs 4,475 per tola from Rs. 4,510 per tola on the previous day.

title: Jetour T2 i-DM Records 1,000+ Bookings in Pakistan Within Days
content:
Chinese automotive brand Jetour has achieved a strong market debut in Pakistan, with its newly launched Jetour T2 i-DM securing more than 1,000 bookings within days of opening orders.

The SUV’s plug-in hybrid technology, rugged styling, and premium features have attracted significant customer interest, reflecting the growing demand for modern SUVs in South Asia and reinforcing Jetour’s expanding global presence.

title: Electrical Short Circuit Sparks Fatal Fire at Thailand Pub; 27 Dead and 63 Injured
content:
A devastating fire broke out at a popular live music pub called Rong Beer Na Lat Phrao in northern Bangkok, Thailand, just before midnight on Sunday. The blaze, which officials believe started from an electrical short circuit near the front stage, quickly ignited the venue's foam soundproofing ceiling and filled the entire building with thick, toxic black smoke. Firefighters managed to bring the flames under control within 30 minutes, but the fast moving smoke created a deadly trap for the crowded room.

At least 27 people have died, and 63 others were injured, with 22 remaining in critical condition at local hospitals. Government officials noted that the high death toll was largely due to smoke inhalation and blocked escape routes. Because the fire started near the main front stage, panicked customers rushed to the back of the building toward the restrooms to escape. However, evacuation efforts failed because both emergency exits were partially blocked by tables and stacked beer crates, trapping many inside. Relatives are currently waiting outside the venue as forensic teams work to identify the victims.

title: Healthy Home Marks 21-Year Journey, Announces Future Growth and Innovation
content:
Healthy Home, Nepal's pioneering holistic health and wellness center, has celebrated its 21st anniversary, marking over two decades of service to more than 50,000 clients through its four branches in Kathmandu and Pokhara. Founded by Mohan Bhusal and Krishna Bhattarai, the company has established itself as a leader in weight management, body shaping, and skin and hair analysis.

During the anniversary celebration, the management reaffirmed its commitment to expanding services, introducing new technologies, and making quality wellness solutions more accessible across Nepal. The company also launched a special anniversary campaign featuring lucky draw prizes, including a cash prize of NPR 100,000, to thank its customers for their continued trust and support.

title: Sanskriti International School Partners with Community School to Offer Free FAB Lab Education
content:
Sanskriti International School has partnered with Shree Panchakanya Secondary School to provide free FAB Lab classes for community school students under its social responsibility initiative.

The year-long program, launched on July 12, 2026, offers hands-on training in 3D printing, coding, AI, robotics, photography, VFX, and other STEAM subjects at Sanskriti's state-of-the-art FAB Lab.

Conducted through fortnightly sessions with expert instructors and free learning materials, the initiative aims to bridge the digital divide and equip students with practical, 21st-century skills while encouraging innovation and community-focused projects.

title: Muktinath Bikas Bank Supports Health and Community Welfare Through CSR Initiative
content:
Muktinath Bikas Bank Limited (MNBBL) has provided wheelchairs, medical equipment, and safe drinking water facilities to hospitals, health posts, schools, and local governments under its Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) program.

The bank distributed 26 wheelchairs to healthcare institutions and local bodies in Salyan, Morang, Rupandehi, and Dang, along with chairs for Pathari Municipal Hospital, electric water dispensers for Madan Bhandari Hospital and Trauma Centre, and water purifiers for a school in Salyan.

The bank said it will continue implementing CSR initiatives focused on healthcare, education, social inclusion, and sustainable community development.

title: Kajaria Ramesh Tiles Opens Exclusive Showroom in Morang, Expands Retail Network
content:
Kajaria Ramesh Tiles Limited has expanded its presence in Eastern Nepal by opening a new exclusive showroom in Kanchanbari, Morang, in partnership with Annapurna Bathware and Marbles.

Inaugurated by Director Himanshu Agrawal and Deepshikha Nepal, the showroom offers a wide range of premium wall tiles, floor tiles, sanitary products, and interactive displays to help customers choose suitable designs.

The company said the expansion aims to provide homeowners and builders in Morang with easier access to high-quality, durable, and sustainable tile solutions.

With more than 100 showrooms across Nepal and over 3,000 tile designs, Kajaria continues to strengthen its nationwide footprint following its recent NS Quality Award 2026 recognition.

title: Nepal Hearing and Speech Clinic Marks 15 Years of Service, Completes 40,000+ Hearing Tests
content:
Nepal Hearing and Speech Clinic has completed 15 years of providing hearing and speech care services in Nepal.

Over the past decade and a half, the clinic has conducted more than 40,000 hearing tests, 25,000 hearing aid fittings, and over 60 cochlear implant services.

Headquartered in Thapathali, Kathmandu, with branches in Biratnagar, Pokhara, and Nepalgunj, the clinic also operates audiology setups at several hospitals.

As the authorized distributor of Phonak, Signia, and Starkey hearing aids, it offers comprehensive hearing solutions backed by advanced international-standard audiology facilities.

Led by founder Purushottam Bhurtel, the clinic plans to further enhance accessible, patient-focused hearing and speech care through modern technology.

title: Janata Agro Moves Closer to IPO, Appoints Siddhartha Capital as Issue Manager
content:
Janata Agro and Forestry Limited has approved key decisions to accelerate its Initial Public Offering (IPO) process, including accepting a total institutional investment of Rs 5 crores from Peoples Hydropower Company Limited and Summit Energy Solution Pvt. Ltd. at Rs 125 per share.

The company also authorized its board to proceed with issuing 20–27.5% of its shares to the public and amended its bylaws to strengthen corporate governance by including independent directors and public shareholder representation.

With Siddhartha Capital appointed as the issue manager and CARE Ratings as the credit rating agency, the company aims to expand its integrated agriculture and forestry business while supporting farmers, promoting local production, reducing import dependency, and creating long-term sustainable value.

title: Department of Passport Launches New System After Major Data Migration
content:
The Department of Passport has successfully upgraded its services by launching a new passport production and management system. As part of this major update, the department safely transferred the passport records of nearly 10 million Nepali citizens into the new system. To complete this massive data shift, passport services were temporarily suspended for two days in last week.

Following the launch of the new system, officials have informed that users might experience initial technical issue, slower processing times, or minor delays in getting their passports.

During this initial transition period, the department is requesting that only individuals who urgently require a passport submit their applications. This will help reduce pressure on the system while it stabilizes. Additionally, the public is advised to rely only on official notices published directly by the department or the Ministry of Foreign Affairs for accurate updates.

title: Hyundai Nepal Concludes Inaugural Next Gen Cup to Promote Grassroots Football
content:
Laxmi Intercontinental Pvt. Ltd., the authorized distributor of Hyundai vehicles in Nepal, successfully organized the inaugural Hyundai Next Gen Cup in partnership with AG10 Academy to promote grassroots football. The one-day Under-16 tournament, held at Chyasal Ground in Lalitpur, featured 80 young players across eight teams representing Hyundai vehicle models.

Team Venue won the championship, while Team Creta Electric finished as runners-up. As an Official Partner of the FIFA World Cup 2026, Hyundai Nepal launched the initiative under its global vision, "Next Starts Now," to provide young footballers with opportunities to develop their skills and confidence. The event also reaffirmed Hyundai Nepal's long-term commitment to youth development and the sustainable growth of football in Nepal through its partnership with AG10 Academy.

title: Daraz Nepal Launches ‘Fast Delivery’ Service for Faster Nationwide Shipping
content:
Daraz Nepal has introduced Fast Delivery, a new nationwide service that enables eligible products to be delivered more quickly at no additional cost to buyers or sellers. Launched on July 10, 2026, the service combines improved seller fulfilment with Daraz's logistics network to deliver faster, more reliable service.

At launch, nearly 50 percent of all orders on the platform qualify for Fast Delivery, including groceries, everyday essentials, electronics, and fashion. More than 2,500 sellers already meet the programme's performance standards.

Products qualify based on a 90-day performance evaluation, requiring timely order dispatch and at least 80 percent of deliveries to reach customers within 48 hours. Eligible products currently average a delivery time of 42.74 hours.

Customers can easily identify qualifying items through a Fast Delivery tag, dedicated search filters, and more accurate estimated delivery dates. Daraz said the initiative is part of its continued investment in logistics, technology, and seller support to strengthen Nepal's growing e-commerce ecosystem and improve the online shopping experience.

title: Rastriya Banijya Bank Signs Banking Agreement with Karnali Provincial Hospital
content:
Rastriya Banijya Bank Limited (RBB) has signed an agreement with the Karnali Province Government's Ministry of Social Development and Province Hospital, Birendranagar, Surkhet, to provide salary savings accounts and Professional Easy Loan facilities to hospital employees.

Under the agreement, RBB will also offer staff a range of banking services at concessional rates. Established in 2022 BS, the fully government-owned bank currently operates 316 branches across all 77 districts of Nepal, providing loans, deposits, ATMs, mobile banking, and other modern digital banking services.

title: MetLife Nepal Partners with Tribhuvan University to Support Actuarial Education
content:
MetLife Nepal has partnered with the School of Mathematical Sciences (SMS), Tribhuvan University, to launch a merit-based scholarship programme aimed at developing Nepal's next generation of actuarial professionals. Under the initiative, two outstanding undergraduate students will receive scholarships covering their semester fees throughout the four-year programme.

Beyond financial support, the partnership will provide internships, practical learning opportunities, and industry exposure to help students build careers in actuarial science, risk management, and insurance. The initiative seeks to address the shortage of actuarial professionals in Nepal while strengthening the country's insurance and financial services sector. MetLife Nepal said the programme reflects its commitment to expanding educational opportunities and supporting the long-term growth of Nepal's financial ecosystem.

title: Frustrated Share Investors Protest Outside SEBON Office Demanding Policy Action
content:
Share Market of Nepal has been falling since the new government take charge, in 100 days of the new government NEPSE falls more than300 points.

Frustrated by a continuous decline in the stock market, share investors gathered outside the central office of the Securities Board of Nepal (SEBON) today in the morning around 9 am  to launch a formal protest. Led by prominent investor and activist Tilak Koirala, the demonstration highlighted the growing anxiety among ordinary investors who feel vulnerable to the market's ongoing downward spiral. The protest is on going in front of the SEBON buildings.

The protestors surrounded the regulator's headquarters (SEBON), calling for immediate and effective decision making from both the government and regulatory authorities to address the market's current distress. The demonstrators urged authorities to end their apparent indifference toward the stock market and demanded robust, protective measures to shield everyday investors from further financial damage.

In anticipation of the protest, security was heavily tightened around the SEBON premises after activist groups threatened to lock down the office gates. Security personnel locked the main entrance to prevent any unauthorized entry, while a significant deployment of Nepal Police officers kept watch inside and outside the perimeter to maintain order and prevent any escalation.

Yesterday NEPSE has fallen by 30.60 points and today the market is on the same way of downtrend, as of writing market falls by 8.41 points today.

title: Gold Price Falls Moderately; Check Today's Updated Rate
content:
Gold prices have witnessed a moderate fall in the domestic market today, falling by Rs. 1,200 per tola to settle at Rs.284,000 per tola, down from the previous rate of Rs. 285,200 per tola.

TheFederation of Nepal Gold and Silver Dealers' Association (FENEGOSIDA)reported that Tejabi gold also decreases moderately by Rs. 1,200 per tola to reach Rs. 283,300 per tola, compared to Rs. 284,500 per tola on the previous day.

Similarly, silver prices move in the same direction, price falls by Rs. 15 per tola, bringing it to Rs.4,360 per tola from Rs. 4,375 per tola on the previous day.

title: Nepal Rasta Bank to Issue Bonds Worth of Rs. 15 Billion with 1 Year Maturity Period
content:
The central bank bonds worth Rs. 15 billion will be issued for a duration of one year. According to the notice published by the central bank's Monetary Management Department, the bond named "NRB Bond 2084 'Ja'" will be issued on Asar 30, 2083.

Only licensed Class ‘A’, ‘B’, and ‘C’ banks and financial institutions are eligible to participate in the bidding process.

The interest rate of the bond will be determined through the bidding process, and the interest earnings will be paid out to investors every six months. The total principal amount will be paid back upon maturity a year later on Asar 30, 2084.

These bonds can be used as collateral for loans at NRB or other financial institutions. While the invested amount cannot be used to meet mandatory cash reserve rules, it can be counted toward statutory liquidity ratio requirements. The central bank also warned that if a winning institution fails to have sufficient funds in its account on the issuance date, it will face a 2.50 percent financial penalty and a six month ban from participating in future open market bidding processes.

title: eSewa Expands Digital Health Insurance with Coverage Up to Rs 600,000
content:
eSewa, in partnership with Shikhar Insurance Company, is expanding access to affordable healthcare through Sahara Health Insurance, with premiums starting from Rs 1,399 and combined health and accident coverage of up to Rs 600,000. Available entirely through the eSewa app, the policy can be purchased or renewed digitally within minutes without paperwork.

The insurance offers medical coverage of up to Rs 500,000, accident treatment benefits of up to Rs 100,000, cashless treatment at more than 25 network hospitals, and coverage for nearly 150 daycare procedures. Since its launch in September 2025, more than 10,000 customers have enrolled in the scheme. eSewa said the initiative reflects its commitment to expanding digital financial services and making health insurance more accessible across Nepal.

title: Samsung Launches Galaxy A27 5G in Nepal with AI Features and 24-Month EMI
content:
amsung has launched the Galaxy A27 5G in Nepal, expanding its Galaxy A series with a smartphone featuring Awesome Intelligence (AI), a 6.7-inch FHD+ Super AMOLED 120Hz display, Snapdragon 6 Gen 3 processor, 50MP OIS camera, 5,000mAh battery, Android 16 with One UI 8.5, and Samsung Knox Vault security. The device also supports AI tools such as Google Gemini, Perplexity, and Voice Transcription.

The Galaxy A27 5G is available in 6GB/128GB and 8GB/512GB variants, priced at NPR 55,999 and NPR 69,999, respectively. Samsung is also offering 24-month EMI plans starting from NPR 1,519 per month, an exchange bonus of up to NPR 8,000, and 120GB of free Ncell data, making the device more accessible to customers in Nepal.

title: Shangrila Development Bank Supports Free Cataract Surgeries for Nine in Darchula
content:
Under its Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) programme, Shangrila Development Bank Limited (SADBL) supported free cataract surgeries for nine patients from Darchula at Geta Eye Hospital in Dadeldhura.

The beneficiaries were identified during free eye camps held at Darchula District Hospital and Dunhu Rural Municipality in Baishakh 2083.

Although 11 patients were referred for surgery, only nine underwent the procedure due to health-related issues affecting the remaining two.

During the eye camps, more than 700 people received free eye check-ups, while 487 were provided with spectacles and 600 received free medicines. The bank currently serves customers through 109 branches and 30 ATMs across Nepal and reaffirmed its commitment to expanding quality banking services while continuing impactful CSR initiatives.

title: Macroeconomic Summary of First 11 Months: Inflation at 5.22 %, Remittances Rises by 38.2%, Foreign Currency Enough to Cover 19.1 Months Import
content:
Nepal Rastra Bank, the central bank of Nepal, has unveiled the current macroeconomic and financial situation of Nepal based on Eleven -month data (Ending Mid-June) of FY 2025/26.

Nepal Rastra Bank estimated that the inflation remained at 5.22 percent on a year-over-year basis. The gross foreign exchange reserves stood at 3755.64 billion in USD terms, whereas the total imports and exports increased 12.3 percent and 15.2 percent, respectively.

NEPSE index stood 2724.03 in mid-June 2026 compared to 2655.39 in mid- June 2025.

The y-o-y wholesale price inflation stood at 8.47 percent in mid-June 2026 compared to 1.56 percent a year ago.

The y-o-y wholesale price inflation of consumption goods decreased 1.00 percent while intermediate goods and capital goods stood at 14.64 percent and 4.78 percent respectively. The yo-y wholesale price index of construction material increased 3.09 percent in the review month.

During the eleven months of 2025/26, merchandise exports increased 12.3 percent to Rs.277.97 billion compared to a growth of 77.8 percent in the same period of the previous year. Destination-wise, exports to India and other countries increased 13.6 percent and 9.2 percent respectively whereas exports to China decreased 35.8 percent. Exports of soyabean oil, palm oil, cardamom, noodles and brans among others increased whereas exports of zinc sheet, particle board, tea, woolen carpet and handicraft goods among others decreased in the review period.

During the eleven months of 2025/26, mercandise imports increased 15.2 percent to Rs. 1894.10 billion compared to a growth of 13.1 percent a year ago. Destination-wise, imports from India, China, and other countries increased 11.8 percent, 21.5 percent, and 18.9 percent respectively. Imports of petroleum products, silver, transport equipment, vehicle and spare parts, chemical fertilizer, and crude soyabean oil among others increased whereas imports of edible oil, hot rolled sheet in coil, garlic, M.S. wire rod, bars, coils and M.S. billet among others decreased in the review period.

Total trade deficit increased 15.7 percent to Rs.1616.13 billion during the eleven months of2025/26. Such a deficit had increased 6.3 percent in the corresponding period of the previous year.The export-import ratio decreased to 14.7 percent in the review period from 15.1 percent in the corresponding period of the previous year.

Net services income remained at a deficit of Rs.72.54 billion during the review period. Such income had a deficit of Rs.82.78 billion in the same period of the previous year.

Remittance inflows increased 38.2 percent to Rs.2120.80 billion in the eleven months of 2025/26 compared to an increase of 15.6 percent in the same period of the previous year. During mid- May to mid-June (Jestha month), remittance inflows stood at Rs. 203.89 billion. In the same period of the previous year, such inflows were Rs. 176.32 billion.

In the review period, interbank transactions of the BFIs amounted Rs.1039.76 billion on a turnover basis, including Rs.893.41 billion among commercial banks and Rs.146.35 billion among other financial institutions (excluding transactions among commercial banks). In the corresponding period of the previous year, such transaction was Rs.1,681.54 billion, including Rs.1,531.21 billion among commercial banks and Rs.150.33 billion among other financial institutions.

The price of oil (Crude Oil Brent) in the international market increased 16.63 percent to US Dollar 88.64 per barrel in mid-June 2026 from US Dollar 76.0 per barrel a year ago. The price of gold increased 21.85 percent to US Dollar 4185.95 per ounce in mid-June 2026 from US Dollar 3435.35 per ounce a year ago.

Gross foreign exchange reserves increased 40.3 percent to Rs.3755.64 billion in mid-June 2026 from Rs.2677.68 billion in mid-July 2025. In US Dollar terms, the gross foreign exchange reserves increased 26.5 percent to 24.68 billion in mid-June 2026 from 19.50 billion in mid-July 2025.

Of the total foreign exchange reserves, the reserves held NRB increased 37.9 percent to Rs3330.07 billion in mid-June 2026 from Rs. 2414.64 billion in mid-July 2025. Reserves held by banks and financial institutions (except NRB) increased 61.8 percent to Rs.425.57 billion in mid -June 2026 from Rs.263.04 billion in mid-July 2025. The share of Indian currency in total reserves stood at 21.5 percent in mid-May 2026.

Based on the imports of the eleven months of 2025/26, the foreign exchange reserves of thebanking sector is sufficient to cover the prospective merchandise imports of 22.5 months, andmerchandise and services imports of 19.1 months. The ratio of reserves-to-GDP, reserves to imports and reserves-to-M2 stood at 61.5 percent, 159.5 percent, and 43.7 percent respectively in mid-June 2026. Such ratios were 43.8 percent, 128.1 percent, and 34.1 percent respectively in midJuly 2025.

Nepalese currency vis-à-vis the US Dollar depreciated 9.8 percent in mid-June 2026 from mid-July 2025. It had depreciated 3.0 percent in the same period of the previous year. The buying exchange rate per US Dollar stood at Rs. 151.88 in mid-June 2026, compared to Rs. 137.0 in mid-July 2025.

According to the Ministry of Finance, Financial Comptroller General Office (FCGO), the total expenditure of the Government of Nepal stood at Rs. 1346.65 billion during the eleven months of 2025/26. Recurrent expenditure, capital expenditure, and financial expenditure amounted to Rs.908.27 billion, Rs. 132.67 billion, and Rs. 305.72 billion, respectively, during the review period.

Cash Balance at various accounts of the GoN maintained with NRB remained Rs. 418.54 billion (including Provincial Governments and Local Government Account) in mid-June 2026. Such a balance was Rs. 137.78 billion in mid-July 2025.

Domestic credit increased by 0.5 percent in the review period compared to an increase of 3.4percent in the corresponding period of the previous year. On y-o-y basis, domestic credit increased by 3.1 percent in mid-June 2026.

Monetary sector's claims on the private sector increased by 7.0 percent in the review periodcompared to an increase of 8.7 percent in the corresponding period of the previous year. On y-o-y basis, such claims increased by 6.4 percent in mid-June 2026.

Deposits at Banks and Financial Institutions (BFIs) increased by 10.3 percent (Rs.748.62 billion) reaching Rs.8,012.49 billion in the review period compared to an increase of 8.0 percent (Rs.517.60 billion) in the corresponding period of the previous year. On y-o-y basis, deposits at BFIs expanded 15.0 percent in mid-June 2026.

Private sector credit from the BFIs increased by 6.2 percent (Rs.340.57 billion) to Rs. 5,838.27 billion in the review period compared to an increase of 8.0 percent (Rs.407.62 billion) in the corresponding period of previous year. On y-oy basis, credit to the private sector from the BFIs increased by 6.5 percent in mid-June 2026.

The average base rate of commercial banks, development banks and finance companies stood at 4.88 percent, 6.86 percent, and 7.16 percent, respectively, in mid-June 2026. The average base rate of commercial banks, development banks and finance companies were 6.09 percent, 8.29 percent, and 9.02 percent respectively in the corresponding montha year ago.

Balance of Payments (BoP) remained at a surplus of Rs.926.06 billion in the review period. Such surplus was Rs.491.44 billion in the previous year. In US Dollar terms, the BoP remained in a surplus of Rs. 6.39 billion in the review period, compared to a surplus of Rs. 3.62 billion in the same period of the previous year.

title: Chitwan Administration Urges Public to Avoid Non-Essential Travel Amid Heavy Rain
content:
The District Administration Office (DAO), Chitwan, has urged the public to avoid non-essential travel due to the heightened risk of landslides and flooding caused by continuous rainfall.

In a public notice, the administration specifically advised travelers to refrain from unnecessary journeys along the Narayangadh–Muglin road section and the Prithvi Highway, where persistent rain has increased the likelihood of landslides and flash floods.

The notice, issued by Assistant Chief District Officer Chiranjivi Sharma, requested travelers to stay in close coordination with nearby security agencies, local governments, the Division Road Office, and other relevant authorities before undertaking any essential travel.

A meeting of the District Disaster Management Committee held on Monday also appealed to commuters to remain vigilant, warning that landslides could disrupt traffic at multiple locations along major highways, including the Narayangadh–Muglin section.

The committee instructed security agencies, the Division Road Office, irrigation offices, and water resources projects to remain on standby with the necessary machinery and equipment for emergency response.

According to the committee, Tuin Khola and Seti Dobhan have been identified as highly vulnerable landslide-prone areas. It also directed authorities to clean and prepare the old emergency shelters previously used by the former District Development Committee and the Nepal Red Cross Society since 2058 BS so they are ready to accommodate people if required.

The administration has further urged residents living along riverbanks and other vulnerable areas to remain on high alert and take necessary precautions throughout the monsoon period.

title: New Motorable Bridge Over Thini Rivulet Boosts Connectivity in Mustang
content:
A new motorable bridge has been completed over the Thini rivulet in Ward No. 5 of Gharapjhong Rural Municipality, Mustang, significantly improving road connectivity for local residents.

Constructed by the Infrastructure Development Office, Mustang, under the Gandaki Provincial Government, the bridge was completed ahead of its scheduled deadline. The project, which also included the completion of the Gharapjhong Ring Road, was finalized during the current fiscal year.

Although the contract had set August 5 as the completion deadline, the contractor, Amit Ashesh JV Construction, finished the work in advance. The bridge project had originally been planned for completion within two years.

According to Suresh Pandey, an engineer at the Infrastructure Development Office, the bridge forms a key part of the ring road connecting all five wards of Gharapjhong Rural Municipality. He also noted that the long-delayed Gharapjhong Ring Road, whose deadline had been extended six times due to various challenges, has finally been completed during the current fiscal year.

The completion of both the bridge and the ring road has made travel easier and more convenient for residents across the rural municipality, improving year-round accessibility and local transportation.

The newly constructed motorable bridge measures 35 meters in length and 8.4 meters in width and was built at a total cost of Rs. 44.6 million. It is expected to strengthen regional connectivity while supporting economic activities and tourism in Mustang.

title: SEBON Unveils Capital Market Development Blueprint 2083, Sets Vision for a Modern and Globally Competitive Market
content:
The Securities Board of Nepal (SEBON) has unveiled its Capital Market Development Blueprint 2083 along with its Policy and Programme for Fiscal Year 2083/84, outlining an ambitious long-term strategy to transform Nepal's capital market into a transparent, technology-driven, competitive, and internationally recognized financial system.

The blueprint envisions shifting Nepal's capital market from being merely a complementary financing mechanism to the banking sector into a strategic pillar of national economic development. It seeks to mobilize domestic and foreign savings into productive sectors such as infrastructure, hydropower, manufacturing, agriculture, information technology, innovation, and entrepreneurship.

One of the major priorities of the new policy is enhancing investor protection and market integrity.

SEBON announced that it will introduce stricter monitoring of so-called "finfluencers," social media personalities and online content creators who attempt to influence stock prices or provide unauthorized investment advice. The regulator plans to curb misleading market promotions and strengthen oversight of digital platforms to prevent market manipulation.

The board also plans to tighten the Initial Public Offering (IPO) approval process. Companies seeking to issue shares will undergo more rigorous evaluations covering their financial health, governance practices, business viability, and managerial competence before receiving regulatory approval.

In addition, SEBON will regulate unauthorized public fundraising activities conducted through social media before IPO issuance, preventing companies from collecting money from investors without proper regulatory approval.

To encourage greater participation in the capital market, SEBON plans to review and reduce various fees charged in both the primary and secondary markets.

The regulator will also introduce a new benchmark stock index based on free-float shares, replacing reliance on broader market capitalization-based indicators. The move is expected to provide a more realistic picture of market performance and improve transparency for investors.

SEBON has pledged to fully operationalize margin trading in coordination with Nepal Rastra Bank (NRB) and the Nepal Stock Exchange (NEPSE). The initiative is expected to improve market liquidity, increase trading activity, and provide investors with additional financing options.

The Capital Market Development Blueprint outlines a comprehensive three-phase national roadmap:

1. Phase I (2026–2027): Legal, institutional, and regulatory reforms.2. Phase II (2028–2030): Market expansion, diversification of financial products, and digital transformation.3. Phase III (2031–2036): Internationalization, regional integration, and development of a globally competitive capital market.

The strategy also includes reforms in collective investment schemes, reviews of mutual fund lock-in periods and allocation mechanisms, stronger inter-agency coordination in securities crime investigations, and modernization of market infrastructure.

SEBON has set several ambitious targets to be achieved by 2036, including:1. Increasing market capitalization to more than 150% of GDP, up from the current level of around 70–80%.2. Expanding the number of listed companies from nearly 300 to over 500.3. Raising average daily stock market turnover from around Rs 8 Arba to Rs 30 Arba.4. Increasing Demat accounts from approximately 80 lakhs to more than 1.20 crores.5. Raising institutional investor participation to over 40% of total market trading.6. Expanding the corporate bond market to account for 20% of the capital market.7. Increasing mutual fund schemes from 56 to more than 150.8. Introducing listings for over 200 Small and Medium Enterprises (SMEs).9. Promoting regular issuance of Green Bonds.10. Gradually allowing foreign portfolio investment through institutional participation.

SEBON emphasized that a well-functioning capital market is a key national mechanism for converting public savings into productive investments. The board noted that developed capital markets are widely recognized by institutions such as the World Bank, International Monetary Fund (IMF), and OECD as fundamental drivers of economic growth, capital formation, industrialization, productivity, and investment expansion.

According to SEBON, Nepal's capital market has evolved significantly over the past three decades with the establishment of SEBON, NEPSE, CDS and Clearing Limited (CDSC), Demat accounts, the Mero Share system, ASBA, and online trading platforms. However, the next stage of development will focus not only on expanding the size of the market but also on improving its quality, governance, technological capability, and international competitiveness.

The long-term vision is built around seven strategic priorities:i. Strengthening the legal and regulatory frameworkii. Deepening and diversifying the capital marketiii. Expanding institutional investorsiv. Building a technology-driven securities marketv. Enhancing investor protection and financial literacyvi. Promoting internationalization and foreign investmentvii. Supporting innovation, SME financing, and sustainable finance

SEBON stated that these reforms are intended to transform Nepal's capital market into a reliable, transparent, inclusive, and technology-oriented financial system capable of supporting sustainable economic growth and attracting both domestic and international investment.

title: Promoter Shares of NIC Asia Bank Limited in Auction From Today
content:
The existing promoters ofNIC Asia Bank Limited (NICA)are selling 45,000 units of promoter shares in an auction to the  general public/institutions.

45,000 units of promoter shares will be auctioned. Individual investors, companies, and institutions are all eligible to bid in an auction. Interested individuals are requested to bid for the auction within 7 days, i.e. 6th Shrawan, 2083, from this notice's publication date, i.e., 31st  Ashad, 2083.

Interested investors should submit their bids at the company's central office located at Kamaladi, Kathmandu.

Notice:

title: Government Invites Applications for Chairperson of Advertisement Board
content:
The Ministry of Communications and Information Technology has invited applications for the position of Chairperson of the Advertisement Board. A public notice issued on Wednesday calls on qualified candidates to submit their applications byJuly 24 (Shrawan 8).

According to the ministry, details about the required qualifications, application form, and the documents that applicants must submit are available on its official website.

The Advertisement Board is responsible for managing and regulating the country's advertising sector. It distributes public interest advertisements provided by the Government of Nepal to media organizations in a fair and proportional manner based on approved criteria. The board also oversees advertisements related to the promotion, marketing, and sale of goods and services.

title: Arun Kabeli Power Limited Announces Sale of Shares of Ingwa Hydropower Limited
content:
Arun Kabeli Power Limited (AKPL)has decided to sell 3,00,000 units of shares it holds in Ingwa Hydropower Limited through the secondary market.

The decision has been made per Clause 11 of the "Electricity Regulatory Commission's Directive on Public Offering and Regulation of Shares of Electricity-Related Companies, 2078," which mandates prior notice to the public for such transactions.

This 15-day public notice has been issued to inform the general public about the share sale process as per regulatory requirements. The notice has been published under the authorization of the Board of Directors of Arun Kabeli Power Limited, located in Thapathali, Kathmandu.

Notice:

title: Nepal Rastra Bank Collecting a Massive Rs. 100 Billion Deposits from BFIs Today
content:
Nepal Rastra Bank (NRB) has announced that it will collect deposits worthRs. 100 billionfrom banks and financial institutions through a bidding process today.

According to a notice published today, the central bank will collect the deposits for a period of91days. The bidding will take place on Ashad 31(July 15)through the Online Bidding System at 3 Pm.

The interest rate will be decided through the bidding process. The minimum amount for bidding has been set at Rs. 10 crore, while the maximum limit is (In multiples of) 5 Crore, up to the total issue amount. However, institutions can submit multiple bids within the total announced amount.

Only licensed ‘A’, ‘B’, and ‘C’ class banks and financial institutions approved by NRB are allowed to take part in the bidding. The deposits collected will be returned along with interest onAshoj 28, 2083(October 14, 2026).

title: Gold Price Rises by 800 Per Tola, Check Today's Updated Rate
content:
Gold prices have witnessed a moderate gain in the domestic market today, gaining by Rs. 800 per tola to settle at Rs. 284,800 per tola, up from the previous rate of Rs. 284,000 per tola.

TheFederation of Nepal Gold and Silver Dealers' Association (FENEGOSIDA)reported that Tejabi gold also rises moderately by Rs. 800 per tola to reach Rs.284,100 per tola, compared to Rs. 283,300 per tola on the previous day.

Similarly, silver prices move in the same direction, price rises by Rs. 55 per tola, bringing it to Rs.4,415 per tola from Rs. 4,360 per tola on the previous day.

title: Full-Weight LPG Cylinders Return to Market as Supply Normalizes
content:
Consumers across Nepal will once again be able to purchase full-weight 14.2 kg Liquefied Petroleum Gas (LPG) cylinders from Wednesday, following the normalization of supply from India.

The Nepal Oil Corporation (NOC) has instructed all LPG bottling industries to resume filling and distributing 14.2 kg cooking gas cylinders as per the regular standard, according to NOC spokesperson Manoj Thakur.

The decision comes after supplies from the Indian Oil Corporation (IOC), Nepal's sole fuel supplier, improved significantly, easing the shortages experienced in recent months.

The NOC has also fixed the retail price of a 14.2 kg LPG cylinder at Rs 2,060.

Earlier, the corporation had temporarily introduced 7.1 kg (half-weight) LPG cylinders after disruptions in supply triggered by the geopolitical tensions arising from the US-Iran-Israel conflict, which affected the availability of cooking gas in Nepal.

With supply conditions returning to normal, the NOC expects the resumption of full-weight cylinders to improve market availability and provide greater convenience for households and commercial consumers across the country.

title: Top Bahadur Rayamajhi Sentenced to Four Years in Bhutanese Refugee Scam
content:
Former Deputy Prime Minister and CPN-UML leader Top Bahadur Rayamajhi has been sentenced to four years in prison after being convicted in the high-profile fake Bhutanese refugee scam.

The Kathmandu District Court on Tuesday sentenced Rayamajhi to four years' imprisonment and imposed a fine of Rs 40,000. The verdict was delivered by a single bench of Judge Tej Bahadur Khadka.

Former Home Minister and Nepali Congress leader Bal Krishna Khand was sentenced to two years in prison and fined Rs 20,000. The court found him guilty of abusing public office, forging government documents, committing fraud, and engaging in organized crime.

In the same case, Bhutanese rights activist Tek Nath Rizal was sentenced to two years in prison and fined Rs 20,000 after being convicted of misusing government documents, fraud, and organized crime.

The court also sentenced Keshav Prasad Dulal, Gyanu Bhandari, Sagar Rai, Sandesh Sharma, Dr. Indrajit Rai, Govinda Kumar Chaudhary, Angdeva Sherma, and Tanka Narayan Pandey to four years in prison each, along with fines of Rs 40,000.

Meanwhile, Shamsher Miya, Haribhakta Maharjan, Niranjan Kumar Kharel, and Narendra KC were each sentenced to one year in prison and fined Rs 10,000 in connection with the case.
